\documentclass{arjournal}

% Uncomment for blinded review exports generated by the platform
% \ARJblindreview

\ARJtitle{Title of the Article}
\ARJsubtitle{Optional Subtitle}
\ARJauthor{Author Name}
\ARJaffiliation{Institution / Department}
\ARJemail{author@example.org}
\ARJORCID{0000-0000-0000-0000}
\ARJarticleType{Research Article}
\ARJkeywords{artistic research; multimedia publication; peer review}
\ARJabstract{Write a concise abstract here. Summarize the research question, artistic method, scholarly contribution, and principal findings or outcomes.}
\ARJfunding{State funding or write "None."}
\ARJconflicts{State conflicts of interest or write "None declared."}
\ARJlicense{Preferred publication license, e.g. CC-BY}
\ARJacknowledgements{Optional acknowledgements.}

\begin{document}

\makearjtitle

\section{Introduction}
Write the manuscript in structured plain LaTeX. The platform will parse this file into a canonical document model and generate HTML and PDF outputs.

This template also supports bibliographic references through a separate BibTeX file. For example, artistic research and multimedia scholarship are discussed in prior work \cite{smith2024art}, while practice-based methods are addressed in book-length form \cite{brown2022practice}.

\section{Images}
Use the image macro for figures. The referenced image file should also be uploaded to the platform as a managed asset.

\ARJfigure{example-image-a}{Example image caption.}{Alternative text describing the image for accessibility and parsing.}

Visual and media-based analysis may also draw on chapter-based scholarship such as \cite{lee2023sound}.

\section{Video}
Do not attempt to embed video directly in the PDF as the primary publication method. Instead, reference the uploaded video asset through a placeholder macro.

\ARJvideo{performance1.mp4}{Performance documentation showing the central gesture in the work.}{poster1.jpg}{performance1_transcript.vtt}

A cited online media resource can also be included in the bibliography, for example \cite{video_example}.

\section{Audio}
Audio is handled the same way as video: uploaded separately, referenced in the text, rendered in HTML by the platform, and optionally represented in generated PDFs.

\ARJaudio{soundscape1.wav}{Soundscape excerpt used in analysis.}{soundscape1_transcript.txt}

Where relevant, datasets may also be cited, as in \cite{dataset_example}.

\section{Tables}
Tables should be authored in LaTeX so they remain structured and machine-readable.

\begin{table}[ht]
\centering
\caption{Example table}
\begin{tabular}{lll}
\toprule
Criterion & Description & Weight \\
\midrule
Originality & Novel contribution & 30\% \\
Rigor & Methodological quality & 30\% \\
Clarity & Communicative strength & 20\% \\
Fit & Journal relevance & 20\% \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\section{References}
Use your preferred bibliography workflow, provided it compiles cleanly and can be parsed by the platform.

\bibliographystyle{plain}
\bibliography{arjournal_references_template}

\ARJprintdeclarations

\end{document}
